title: Commentary on Key Financial Performance – December 31, 2026
content:
The credit union financial position continues to be supported by exceptionally strong capital levels. The credit union reports a net worth ratio of 10.33%, representing an increase of 4 bps from the prior period, and a solvency ratio of 1.10. This robust buffer provides ample room for absorbing potential losses while maintaining regulatory standing. These elevated capital metrics enable continued investment in member services without compromising financial resilience.

Asset quality remains stable and manageable. The delinquency ratio stands at 0.34% of total loans, while net charge-offs stand at 0.22% of average loans. Delinquency improved by 10 bps from the prior period, reflecting disciplined collection efforts across all loan portfolios. Continued monitoring will be essential to sustain this trajectory amid evolving economic conditions.

Profitability remains positive. The credit union reports a return on average assets (ROAA) of 0.73% and a net interest margin of 3.35%, both increased by 9.88 bps and 27.04 bps respectively, from the prior period. Improved yield performance on earning assets drove NIM expansion, while cost discipline supported overall profitability. Net operating expenses to average assets decreased by -6.98% relative to the prior year.

Balance-sheet structure continues to reflect a well-capitalized operating profile. Loans represent 78.75% of shares, with loan balances increased by 5.47%, while shares and deposits grew by 4.99%, reflecting continued balance-sheet expansion. The core funding ratio stands at 24.68%, demonstrating strong liquidity positioning that supports both member needs and regulatory expectations.

Overall, the credit union enters Q1'2026 with solid capital adequacy, healthy asset quality, and resilient profitability underpinning its growth trajectory. Its primary strength lies in maintaining high-quality assets alongside disciplined expense management. Sustained performance will depend on preserving these fundamentals while navigating external market dynamics.
